context.project_description: A system that tracks LLM token usage per query for cost monitoring and optimization purposes, integrating with existing LLM provider interfaces to capture input/output tokens from all supported providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama).
key_requirements: Add token usage tracking so I can see how many LLM tokens each query costs
Capture token counts from all LLM provider interfaces (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, etc.)
constraints: No breaking changes to existing APIs must be maintained
Minimal performance impact on LLM inference operations
Support all providers in the stack: OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines protocol interfaces for LLM clients including ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, etc. All provider implementations (openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py) inherit from these protocols and make actual LLM calls via their respective SDKs or HTTP clients.

in_scope: Core tracking: Hook into each ChatProvider.chat() and similar methods (embed, etc.)

existing_files: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls.
fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance.
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/token_tracker.py — Centralized logic for collecting, aggregating, and exposing token usage statistics per query
fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked.py — Wrapper module that wraps all provider classes with token counting logic using the instrumentation system

assumptions.assumption: Tracking happens at per-provider call level
assumptions.impact: Would miss some edge cases; might undercount if middleware layers wrap calls
Need fallback logic for providers like Ollama that don't expose this by default
If user expects structured API response instead, we'd have to change how data is returned

assumptions.assumption: All LLM calls go through protocol interfaces
assumptions.impact: Some low-level internal services might skip provider stack — would require tracing from root

assumptions.assumption: Token counts are additive per model call
assumptions.impact: Could be wrong if a single operation triggers multiple API calls (e.g., batch embedding)

approaches.name: Instrumented Proxy Wrapper with Context-Aware Aggregation
approaches.description: Use fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py's existing InstrumentedProxy to wrap all LLM provider methods (chat, embed, etc.) and inject token counting hooks. Each wrapped method logs input/output tokens into a thread-local or query-scoped context manager.
pros: Seamlessly tracks tokens in all providers without modifying their interfaces
Integrates cleanly with existing hook system for observability
Leverages existing tooling rather than creating new infrastructure
cons: Requires careful configuration of InstrumentedProxy wrapping logic
Logging infrastructure must support querying by query ID
Failure mode if instrumentation system is not properly configured or multiple threads access shared contexts without proper isolation
best_for: Need to track tokens across multiple LLM providers consistently

approaches.name: Decorator-Based Token Hook Injection
approaches.description: Add a token-counting decorator (e.g., @track_tokens) that wraps LLM methods directly inside each provider class. Decorators capture input/output tokens using the underlying SDK client APIs, then store them in thread-local state or request context.
pros: Direct injection at method level allows precise control over what gets tracked where
cons: High operational burden - each provider class needs explicit annotation and could introduce boilerplate
Harder to retrofit if existing implementations already exist
Inconsistent application of decorators across providers may lead to missing tracking for some methods
best_for: Small, controlled systems where manual decoration is acceptable
When provider interfaces are stable and don't change frequently

architecture.recommended: Instrumented Proxy Wrapper with Context-Aware Aggregation
architecture.reasoning: This approach aligns perfectly with the current architecture using fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py. The system already has a robust hooking mechanism that supports benchmarking and caching via InstrumentedProxy, which makes it natural to extend this same pattern for token counting. It avoids patching or decorating existing classes that would require invasive changes, maintains thread safety through context isolation techniques already used in logging/query systems (e.g., via set_query_context()), and scales well as more providers are added since wrapping happens at the interface level rather than specific SDKs.

key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_vs_manual_decorators: Instrumented proxy wrapper avoids manual decorator application and developer discipline requirements, but requires careful configuration of wrapping logic across all provider implementations
key_tradeoffs.centralized_vs_per_method_tracking: Centralized metrics middleware with SDK interception is more fragile to upstream changes but provides deeper integration; context-aware aggregation via InstrumentedProxy offers better stability at cost of slightly more complex setup
key_tradeoffs.performance_impact: Token counting hooks add minimal overhead during LLM calls, but thread-local state management and response parsing introduce small operational complexity

technology_considerations: InstrumentedProxy from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py for proxy-based wrapping of LLM provider methods
threading.local() for thread-safe query-scoped token tracking context isolation
OpenAI SDK response structure parsing with getattr fallbacks for prompt_tokens and completion_tokens fields
Anthropic SDK usage field extraction using input_tokens/output_tokens instead of prompt_tokens/completion_tokens
Cohere SDK metadata parsing from response.meta.tokens structure with graceful degradation handling
Ollama API response header/body parsing for token detection with warning logs when missing
Protocol-based interface contracts (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider) to maintain compatibility across providers while enabling instrumentation at implementation level
architecture.scope_statement: This task produces two modules (token_tracker.py and tracked.py) - the challenge lies in integrating with the instrumentation framework correctly, ensuring proper aggregation of token counts per query across different providers, and not introducing performance regression during inference flows. The solution must support all current LLM provider types (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) while handling their differences in how they expose usage metadata.

adrs.title: ADR: Instrumented Proxy Wrapper with Context-Aware Aggregation for Token Tracking
adrs.context: Need to track input/output tokens from LLM provider calls across different vendors (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) without modifying their interfaces or introducing fragile SDK patching.
adrs.decision: Use fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py's existing InstrumentedProxy framework to wrap concrete LLM provider classes with token counting hooks that extract usage data from SDK responses on a per-query basis using thread-local storage.
adrs.rationale: This approach leverages existing infrastructure, ensures full compatibility across all providers through interface-level wrapping (while instrumenting concrete implementations for token access), maintains query-scoped isolation via threading.local(), and avoids brittle SDK-specific changes or manual decorator application. It also follows established patterns in the codebase like benchmarking and caching hooks.
consequences: Minimal impact on core provider interfaces - all methods return same types
Thread-safe token tracking using existing context management practices (threading.local())
Query-scoped aggregation prevents cross-request contamination of metrics
Requires updating tracked.py to wrap concrete implementations instead of protocols for proper SDK access
Performance overhead is limited to instrumentation layer with minimal impact on inference speed
alternatives_considered: Decorator-Based Token Hook Injection - rejected because it requires manual application across all providers leading to maintenance burden and inconsistency risks

Centralized Metrics Middleware with Provider-Specific SDK Interception - rejected because it introduces fragile tight coupling to external SDK behaviors that can break during version upgrades or API changes

adrs.title: ADR: Robust Token Extraction Logic for Diverse LLM Providers
adrs.context: Different LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) expose token usage data in varying formats and structures within their SDK responses.
adrs.decision: Implement provider-specific fallback logic that safely extracts tokens from SDK response objects with defensive checks for missing fields or structure differences.
adrs.rationale: This ensures the system can gracefully handle different SDK behaviors without crashing when certain providers don't expose token usage, while still capturing available data where possible. The approach uses hasattr() and getattr() with defaults to avoid exceptions on malformed responses.
consequences: Token extraction is robust against missing or differently structured usage fields across providers
Warning logs are emitted for incomplete token data rather than system failures
Codebase becomes more resilient to SDK version changes by not relying on fixed field names
Each provider requires careful attention during implementation and testing due to structural differences
alternatives_considered: Hardcoded Field Access Assumptions - rejected because it would break when SDKs change response formats or when providers don't expose tokens at all (e.g., Ollama base API)
Single Uniform Extraction Logic - rejected because different providers have completely different token exposure patterns (OpenAI vs Anthropic vs Cohere vs Ollama)

adrs.title: ADR: Thread-Safe Per-Query Token Tracking Context
adrs.context: Token tracking must be isolated per query to support concurrent request handling without data corruption or incorrect attribution.
adrs.decision: Use threading.local() storage in the token tracker with thread-local state management that integrates with existing query context systems via set_query_context() and similar logging patterns.
adrs.rationale: This maintains consistency with how other parts of the system manage per-query state (like logging) while providing clean isolation between concurrent requests. It leverages established practices from fitz_ai/logging/logger.py rather than creating new concurrency mechanisms.
consequences: Thread safety is guaranteed through standard library threading.local() mechanism
Per-request token tracking prevents contamination of metrics across different users/queries
Context propagation must be carefully managed in async workflows to preserve correct thread-local state
No global counters or shared mutable state required - keeps system simple and predictable
alternatives_considered: Global Shared Counters with Locks - rejected because it introduces complexity, potential performance bottlenecks, and violates the principle of query-scoped isolation
Process-Level Aggregation with External State Store - rejected because it's overengineered for this use case and adds unnecessary dependency on external systems

components.name: TokenTracker
components.purpose: Central token tracking logic for LLM providers
responsibilities: Record input tokens for a specific model
Record output tokens for a specific model
Aggregate and return total tokens per model

components.name: TrackedProviders
components.purpose: Wrapper module that instruments all LLM provider classes with token counting
responsibilities: Wrap concrete provider classes to inject instrumentation before and after method calls
Extract token usage from SDK responses based on provider-specific patterns
interfaces: OpenAIChatTracked(chat(messages: list[dict[str, Any]], model: str, **kwargs) -> str)
AnthropicChatTracked(chat(messages: list[dict[str, Any]], model: str, **kwargs) -> str)
AnthropicVisionTracked(describe_image(image_path: str, prompt: str, model: str, **kwargs) -> str)
CohereChatTracked(chat(messages: list[dict[str, Any]], model: str, **kwargs) -> str)
OllamaChatTracked(chat(messages: list[dict[str, Any]], model: str, **kwargs) -> str)

integration_points: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — InstrumentedProxy wrapper system used to instrument LLM provider methods with token tracking hooks
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Protocol definitions (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, etc.) that are implemented by concrete providers and need to be wrapped for instrumentation
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — Concrete OpenAI provider implementations (OpenAIChat, OpenAIEmbedding) that require token extraction from SDK responses
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Concrete Anthropic provider implementations (AnthropicChat, AnthropicVision) with different usage field structures requiring specialized extraction logic
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Concrete Cohere provider implementations (CohereChat, CohereEmbedding) that expose token data differently from OpenAI and need custom parsing
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Concrete Ollama provider implementations (OllamaChat, OllamaEmbedding) which have no standardized token fields requiring detection logic

phases.name: Source Code Verification
phases.objective: Verify token usage extraction patterns and infrastructure alignment across all LLM providers to ensure implementation accuracy.
deliverables: List of verified facts from source code review ensuring provider-specific token structures are correctly identified
key_risks: Inconsistent SDK response structures may cause silent failures in token extraction logic
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.token_tracker import TokenTracker; tracker = TokenTracker.get_instance(); print('Verification complete')"

phases.name: Core TokenTracker Implementation
phases.objective: Implement the core token tracking mechanism that aggregates input/output tokens per model within a thread-local context.
deliverables: New file: fitz_ai/llm/token_tracker.py with ThreadLocal-based TokenTracker class and methods
key_risks: Thread safety issues if concurrent queries are not properly isolated using threading.local()
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.token_tracker import TokenTracker; tracker = TokenTracker.get_instance(); tracker.record_input_tokens('gpt-4', 100); tracker.record_output_tokens('gpt-4', 50); assert tracker.get_total_tokens() == {'gpt-4': (100, 50)}; print('TokenTracker working correctly')"

phases.name: Tracked Providers Module Generation
phases.objective: Generate instrumented provider wrappers that extract and track token usage from each supported LLM SDK.
deliverables: New file: fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked.py implementing wrapped providers with custom handlers for token extraction
key_risks: Provider-specific SDK changes could break response parsing logic in _extract_tokens_from_response function
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked import get_tracked_chat_provider, OpenAIChat; from unittest.mock import Mock; mock_provider = Mock(); mock_provider.chat.return_value = type('Response', (), {'usage': Mock(prompt_tokens=100, completion_tokens=50)})(); wrapped = get_tracked_chat_provider(OpenAIChat); result = wrapped.chat(messages=[], model='gpt-4'); print(result)"

phases.name: Integration With LLM Factory
phases.objective: Modify the provider factory to return tracked providers when token tracking is enabled.
deliverables: Updated fitz_ai/llm/factory.py using tracked provider factories for ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider
key_risks: Instrumentation layer might not support safe wrapping of all providers or could introduce double-wrapping errors
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.factory import create_chat_provider; provider = create_chat_provider('openai'); print(type(provider).__name__)"

phases.name: Logging Integration & Query Context Awareness
phases.objective: Integrate token logging into the existing query context and ensure final counts are reported with proper scope.
deliverables: Updated fitz_ai/logging/logger.py including log_query_tokens function that reports current thread's token usage
key_risks: Token counts may leak between concurrent requests if query context management is not precise
phases.verification_command: python -c "import logging; from fitz_ai.logging.logger import get_logger, log_query_tokens; logger = get_logger('test'); log_query_tokens(logger); print('Token logging integration checked.')"

risks.description: Different SDKs expose usage in inconsistent ways. If one provider changes its field structure, it will break the extractor logic.
risks.mitigation: Implement robust error handling with try-except blocks and fallback behavior for missing fields in token extraction functions
risks.contingency: If a provider's usage structure changes, update the corresponding _extract_tokens_from_response function to handle new format while maintaining backward compatibility
risks.verification: Run tests against mocked OpenAI response with missing .usage fields to confirm fallback behavior works

risks.description: If the application uses multiple threads (e.g., async handlers), there's potential for state leakage between concurrent queries unless context management is very precise.
risks.mitigation: Ensure comprehensive thread-local storage testing and implement strict isolation checks using threading.local()
risks.contingency: Add race condition tests to verify token tracking isolation across threads when executing concurrent requests in parallel
risks.verification: Use threading tests to simulate race conditions on token recording and verify isolation with threading.local()

risks.description: The current instrumentation system was built for benchmark hooks, not token counting. Some edge cases might cause method interception or wrapping failures when multiple proxies are stacked.
risks.mitigation: Thoroughly test each concrete provider type being instrumented via maybe_wrap() and implement defensive programming techniques
risks.contingency: If provider wrapping fails, fall back to direct method injection rather than using InstrumentedProxy for that specific provider
risks.verification: Run unit tests on each concrete provider type being instrumented via maybe_wrap() and verify no exceptions occur during wrapping

risks.description: Ollama doesn't have standard usage fields, so we silently skip tracking which could cause silent underreporting of costs for models that don't expose token info.
risks.mitigation: Add explicit logging when skipping Ollama token tracking to ensure visibility into this limitation
risks.contingency: Provide alternative cost estimation mechanisms or warnings in documentation for Ollama users who rely on token counting
risks.verification: Manually inspect a real Ollama API response and confirm no token fields exist; verify fallback logic in _extract_tokens_from_response handles this gracefully

risks.description: OpenAI SDK version changes could affect .usage field structure, breaking our parser silently.
risks.mitigation: Implement flexible parsing logic that can handle variations in OpenAI's response format and add comprehensive test coverage with various structures
risks.contingency: Update token extraction function to be resilient against API schema changes by checking for presence of fields before accessing them
risks.verification: Write a test that mocks various structures of openai.ChatCompletion and ensures all pass through _extract_tokens_from_response

risks.description: Token counts not reset correctly between queries if query contexts aren't managed properly via set_query_context().
risks.mitigation: Implement strict verification in token tracker's reset method and ensure all query execution paths call reset() appropriately
risks.contingency: Add assertion checks that verify isolated token logs per thread by running concurrent tests simulating multiple requests
risks.verification: Run concurrent tests that simulate two requests (e.g., threads) and ensure each has isolated token logs (get_total_tokens)

risks.description: Future OpenAI SDK version changes may alter how usage is reported, breaking our parser without error.
risks.mitigation: Create a wrapper around token extraction logic that can adapt to changing API structures with minimal disruption to functionality
risks.contingency: Implement version-specific parsers or schema validation for OpenAI responses and provide upgrade path documentation
risks.verification: Write integration tests covering different versions of openai.ChatCompletion response objects

recommended_contingencies: Implement comprehensive multi-threaded testing for token tracking isolation
Add explicit logging when skipping Ollama token tracking to maintain visibility into cost estimation gaps
Create version-resilient parsing logic for OpenAI SDK response structures with backward compatibility layer
Ensure all query execution paths properly call TokenTracker.reset() to prevent cross-request contamination

risks.contingency: If a provider's usage structure changes, update the corresponding _extract_tokens_from_response function to handle new format while maintaining backward compatibility
risks.verification: Run tests against mocked OpenAI response with missing .usage fields to confirm fallback behavior works

risks.description: If the application uses multiple threads (e.g., async handlers), there's potential for state leakage between concurrent queries unless context management is very precise.
risks.mitigation: Ensure comprehensive thread-local storage testing and implement strict isolation checks using threading.local()
risks.contingency: Add race condition tests to verify token tracking isolation across threads when executing concurrent requests in parallel
risks.verification: Use threading tests to simulate race conditions on token recording and verify isolation with threading.local()

risks.description: The current instrumentation system was built for benchmark hooks, not token counting. Some edge cases might cause method interception or wrapping failures when multiple proxies are stacked.
risks.mitigation: Thoroughly test each concrete provider type being instrumented via maybe_wrap() and implement defensive programming techniques
risks.contingency: If provider wrapping fails, fall back to direct method injection rather than using InstrumentedProxy for that specific provider
risks.verification: Run unit tests on each concrete provider type being instrumented via maybe_wrap() and verify no exceptions occur during wrapping

risks.description: Ollama doesn't have standard usage fields, so we silently skip tracking which could cause silent underreporting of costs for models that don't expose token info.
risks.mitigation: Add explicit logging when skipping Ollama token tracking to ensure visibility into this limitation
risks.contingency: Provide alternative cost estimation mechanisms or warnings in documentation for Ollama users who rely on token counting
risks.verification: Manually inspect a real Ollama API response and confirm no token fields exist; verify fallback logic in _extract_tokens_from_response handles this gracefully

risks.description: OpenAI SDK version changes could affect .usage field structure, breaking our parser silently.
risks.mitigation: Implement flexible parsing logic that can handle variations in OpenAI's response format and add comprehensive test coverage with various structures
risks.contingency: Update token extraction function to be resilient against API schema changes by checking for presence of fields before accessing them
risks.verification: Write a test that mocks various structures of openai.ChatCompletion and ensures all pass through _extract_tokens_from_response

risks.description: Token counts not reset correctly between queries if query contexts aren't managed properly via set_query_context().
risks.mitigation: Implement strict verification in token tracker's reset method and ensure all query execution paths call reset() appropriately
risks.contingency: Add assertion checks that verify isolated token logs per thread by running concurrent tests simulating multiple requests
risks.verification: Run concurrent tests that simulate two requests (e.g., threads) and ensure each has isolated token logs (get_total_tokens)

risks.description: Future OpenAI SDK version changes may alter how usage is reported, breaking our parser without error.
risks.mitigation: Create a wrapper around token extraction logic that can adapt to changing API structures with minimal disruption to functionality
risks.contingency: Implement version-specific parsers or schema validation for OpenAI responses and provide upgrade path documentation
risks.verification: Write integration tests covering different versions of openai.ChatCompletion response objects

recommended_contingencies: Implement comprehensive multi-threaded testing for token tracking isolation
Add explicit logging when skipping Ollama token tracking to maintain visibility into cost estimation gaps
Create version-resilient parsing logic for OpenAI SDK response structures with backward compatibility layer
Ensure all query execution paths properly call TokenTracker.reset() to prevent cross-request contamination
